extra.reasoning:
syncope appears in exactly one rule, interaction_drop (priority 100), which forces acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity). Because this is the highest-priority rule, enabling syncope can only ever drop acuity, never raise it.

Counterexample (the witness_assignment is the syncope=true state): leg_swelling, stiff_neck, hemoptysis, focal_weakness, fever all true; sweating false.

Baseline (syncope=false, everything else identical): interaction_drop requires syncope, so it does NOT fire. The priority-3 rules raise_focal_weakness and raise_stiff_neck fire, yielding acuity = emergent (priority 3 beats the priority-2 urgent rules from fever/leg_swelling/hemoptysis). Result: emergent.

After setting syncope=true (all else unchanged): all six conjuncts of interaction_drop (leg_swelling AND stiff_neck AND hemoptysis AND focal_weakness AND fever AND syncope) are satisfied. With priority 100 it wins over all other rules, setting acuity = self_care.

Transition emergent -> self_care is a decrease, so setting syncope true lowers acuity, violating the monotonicity property. The witness_assignment gives the post-flip (syncope=true) input that produces self_care, while the same input with syncope=false produces emergent.
